pymetis
  • API Reference
  • engine.core — Tag / Parametrization System
  • View page source

engine.core — Tag / Parametrization System

Internals of the tag-based specialization and promotion system.

class pymetis.engine.core.parametrizable.Parametrizable[source]

Bases: ABC

Abstract base class for all types parametrizable by custom tags (tiny pieces of data, such as short strings or integers).

The goal is to be able to define generic interfaces for internal structures and algorithms and only override the smallest possible subset of functionality in derived classes.

The tags can be defined either - statically inside the class (e.g. all LM recipes and data items should specify band = “LM”

in the class definition). We call this a ‘specialization’ of the class. Note that partial specialization (defining some of the tags while leaving others unspecified) is possible, one might want to define an abstract intermediate class.

  • or obtained from the loaded data in the instances of the class (e.g. target can be either “SKY” or “STD” or “SCI”, but this is not known until the data are loaded). We call this a ‘promotion’ of the class.

The end effect should be the same: tag placeholders ({tag}) will be replaced by the defined values. Only fully specialized classes can be instantiated. This also implies that unlike specialization, promotion must be always complete.

In the context of the pipeline some useful tags may be

  • band: (LM|N|IFU)

  • target: (STD|SCI)

  • detector: (2RG|GEO|IFU)

  • source: (LAMP|TWILIGHT)

Other parameters might be useful for different pipelines. Any strings or types convertible to strings (!s) may be used.

classmethod tag_parameters()[source]

Return the tag parameters for this class. By default, there are none, but mixins may add their own.

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

class pymetis.engine.core.parametrizable.ParametrizableItem[source]

Bases: Parametrizable, ABC

Abstract base class for all items parametrizable by tags, such as data items and QC parameters.

final classmethod find(key)[source]

Try to retrieve the ParametrizableItem subclass with tag key from the global registry.

If not found, return None instead (and leave it to the caller to raise an exception if this is not desired). # ToDo: Maybe we should raise an exception here instead and let the caller handle it?

Return type:

Optional[type[Self]]

Parameters:

key (str)

classmethod specialize(**parameters)[source]

Specialize the data item’s name template with given parameters

Return type:

str

Parameters:

parameters (str)

classmethod name()[source]

Return the machine-oriented name (tag) of the data item as defined in the DRLD, e.g. “DETLIN_2RG_RAW”.

Return type:

str

classmethod description()[source]

Return the human-readable description of the item. By default, this just returns the protected internal attribute, but can be overridden to build the description from other data, such as band or target.

Return type:

str

class pymetis.engine.core.parametrizable.ParametrizableContainer[source]

Bases: Parametrizable, ABC

A ParametrizableContainer is an abstract container class (contains other ParametrizableItem classes) and is itself Parametrizable. If specialized / promoted, it propagates the same operation down to all its attributes of type “Meta._T”.

class Meta[source]

Bases: object

Class for storing class-wide metadata:

_T: the expected type of the inner items.

classmethod list_classes()[source]

List all available inner items

classmethod list_descriptions()[source]

Print formatted descriptions of all available inner items (for man page and such)

Return type:

str

classmethod specialize(**parameters)[source]

Specialize this class statically (class-based, from code).

Return type:

None

classmethod promote(**parameters)[source]

Promote the inner items of this class to appropriate subclasses, as determined from the input data. This may be only called after the recipe is initialized.

May also contain template variables that are not mixed in during class creation. For instance, recipe_{band}_{target} can specify band=LM, but no target, resulting in partial specialization. The target has to be supplied from the actual data then.

Return type:

None

class pymetis.engine.core.format.FormatPlaceholder[source]

Bases: object

__init__(key)[source]
class pymetis.engine.core.format.FormatDict[source]

Bases: dict

pymetis.engine.core.format.partial_format(template, **kwargs)[source]

Partially format a string containing template tags. Values found in the template but not defined are retained as placeholders. Values not found in the template but defined as a kwarg are silently ignored.

partial_format(‘{det}_BLAH_{target}’, det=’GEO’, foo=’abc’) becomes ‘GEO_BLAH_{target}’

Return type:

str

Parameters:

template (str)
